narratives.title: Macro Narrative
narratives.body: Macro-policy sentiment remains watchful rather than shock-grade. The Economic Policy Uncertainty Index cooled to 439.64 as of 2026-05-19, which still keeps policy relevant, but the active seven-day tape has thinned enough to pull the regime back from outright macro-panic language.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Classify whether macro-policy narrative pressure is actively changing soybean-oil procurement timing.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Macro narrative only becomes procurement-relevant when policy fear stays persistent enough to reinforce physical or energy-market stress.
quantResearchProtocol: Track policy-uncertainty level and short-window trend.
Compare narrative pressure with the live lead driver.
Classify the regime as shock-watch or monitor-only.
Translate the regime into buyer urgency language.
inferenceConstraints: Do not call a regime shift from one headline pulse.
Do not ignore conflicting cross-channel evidence.
Do not emit generic sentiment language without buyer impact.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest seven-day macro and policy narrative rows

notes: Macro narrative is anchored to the live uncertainty series plus the active headline window.
The card explicitly separates a series shock from headline-density breadth.

narratives.title: Flow Narrative
narratives.body: The active seven-day tape now holds 5 high-signal rows, with policy tags on 2, energy tags on 2, macro context on 1, and China tags on 0. That means the narrative regime is still concentrated and policy-led, but it is thinner and less self-confirming than yesterday.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Determine whether the current headline mix supports stable price discovery or a fragile narrative regime.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Headline flow becomes dangerous for buyers when thematic density and persistence reinforce each other; sparse mixed headlines mostly raise monitoring needs, not panic action.
quantResearchProtocol: Count active rows and tag density over the last seven days.
Separate total velocity from soy-relevant density.
Classify the tape as sparse, balanced, or crowded.
Tie the result to monitoring frequency.
inferenceConstraints: Do not equate headline count with regime certainty.
Do not overfit one thematic cluster without density confirmation.
Do not suppress uncertainty when the tape is sparse.
outputRequirements: State row count and key tag counts.
Explain whether the tape is sparse or crowded.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest seven-day sentiment rows and tag counts

notes: Flow narrative is density-based rather than hype-based.
Concentrated narrative breadth keeps the cadence tight without pretending the tape is broad.

narratives.title: Procurement Narrative
narratives.body: CFTC remains bullish with managed-money net length of 158,102 contracts, or 22.45% of open interest, while the broad risk network cooled to 36.7 and policy alone still leads at 51.5. That combination still favors disciplined accumulation with faster re-checks instead of one-shot urgency or passive waiting.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Convert sentiment, positioning, and risk-network conditions into a buyer-ready pacing posture.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Buyer outcomes improve when bullish positioning is translated into pacing rules instead of emotional urgency.
quantResearchProtocol: Use CoT bias and managed-money ratio as the positioning anchor.
Cross-check the position signal against the average risk network.
Classify buyer psychology as calm, disciplined, or urgent.
State the pacing rule that follows from the classification.
inferenceConstraints: Do not issue binary buy-or-wait language without uncertainty framing.
Do not ignore asymmetric upside reflex risk when the market is net long.
Do not recommend static cadence in mixed-risk conditions.
outputRequirements: State the trigger that would force a posture change.

sourceRecords.recordHint: current average risk-network score and policy leadership

notes: Procurement narrative combines positioning with network pressure instead of treating either one as sufficient by itself.

positioningFlow.title: Managed Money Positioning
positioningFlow.body: The latest soybean-oil CoT print (2026-05-12) still shows bullish managed-money bias with net length 158,102 contracts and a 22.45% open-interest share. That keeps upside reflex risk alive, but it remains a weekly positioning signal rather than a real-time panic trigger.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Determine whether managed-money positioning is stabilizing or destabilizing soybean-oil procurement expectations.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Large bullish net length can amplify price reflexes even when the broader network is not in crisis.
quantResearchProtocol: Read the latest managed-money net and ratio to open interest.
Classify the bias with a confidence caveat because CoT is weekly.
Cross-map the bias against current volatility and energy conditions.
Translate the result into buyer caution level.
inferenceConstraints: Do not treat weekly CoT as real-time certainty.
Do not equate size with durability of conviction.
outputRequirements: State the bias class and math.
Say what would change the interpretation.

notes: Bias classification is weekly and must be read with freshness discipline.
Output is buyer-facing, not speculative positioning advice.

headlineFlow.title: Headline Flow
headlineFlow.body: Headline velocity is still policy-led, but the tape is thinner: only 5 current high-signal rows are active, with no China-tagged confirmation and just 2 energy-tagged rows. Treat this as a concentrated watch surface, not a broad narrative cascade.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Assess whether the current headline stream is producing actionable signal or just adding narrative noise.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Headline velocity only becomes actionable when it is thematically coherent and persistent enough to reinforce live market pressure.
quantResearchProtocol: Track 24-hour and seven-day headline density.
Identify whether the active tape is energy-led, policy-led, or mixed.
Compare headline concentration with the live market drivers.
Set monitoring cadence from coherence, not count alone.
inferenceConstraints: Do not infer a regime shift from headline velocity alone.
Do not ignore low thematic density.
Do not provide cadence advice without coherence checks.
outputRequirements: State whether the tape is signal or noise dominant.

sourceRecords.recordHint: Farm, Food, and National Security Act current watch item

notes: Headline-flow framing gives more weight to thematic coherence than raw count.
The tape is still too concentrated for regime-calling on headlines alone.
